channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Dodgers set to re-sign outfielder Teoscar Hernández for $66 million over 3 years, AP source says
item.description: Teoscar Hernández is headed back to the Los Angeles Dodgers. The free agent outfielder has agreed with the reigning World Series champions on a $66 million, three-year contract, a person familiar with the negotiations told The Associated Press late Friday night.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kings fire coach Mike Brown less than halfway through his 3rd season
item.description: The Sacramento Kings have fired coach Mike Brown less than halfway through his third season with the team mired in a five-game losing streak. General manager Monte McNair called the move Friday a “difficult decision” and thanked Brown for his work.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stars forward Mason Marchment taken to hospital after getting hit in face by puck
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Stars forward Mason Marchment was helped off the ice during Friday night’s game against the Minnesota Wild and taken to a hospital after being hit in the face by a puck. Dallas coach Pete DeBoer said Marchment is OK, but was not sure how long he’ll be sidelined.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sportscaster Greg Gumbel dies from cancer at age 78
item.description: Greg Gumbel, a longtime CBS sportscaster who broke barriers during his career calling some of the biggest sporting events, has died from cancer, according to a statement from family released by the network on Friday. He was 78.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mets and pitcher Sean Manaea finalize $75 million, 3-year deal
item.description: Sean Manaea and the New York Mets have finalized a $75 million, three-year contract that keeps one of the team’s best pitchers last season at the front of a remodeled rotation.
item.dc:creator: Mike Fitzpatrick, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NBA coaches react with dismay over firing of 2-time coach of the year Mike Brown
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — Not even two years ago, Rick Carlisle publicly lauded Mike Brown for the job he did on the way to winning the NBA's coach of the year award. And on Friday, Carlisle was among the coaches reacting with dismay that Brown was fired.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sportscaster Greg Gumbel dead from cancer at 78
item.description: David Berson, president and CEO of CBS Sports, described Gumbel as breaking barriers and setting standards for others during his years as a voice for fans in sports, including in the NFL and March Madness.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Navy gets 95-yard TD run from Horvath and stops 2-point try to beat Oklahoma in Armed Forces Bowl
item.description: FORT WORTH, Texas (AP) — Blake Horvath set a Navy record with a 95-yard touchdown run and then scored a go-ahead 6-yarder with 4:34 left as the Midshipmen overcame an early two-touchdown deficit and stopped a late 2-point conversion attempt to beat Oklahoma 21-20 in the Armed Forces Bowl on Friday.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Depleted Notre Dame defense hopes to make an impact against Georgia in CFP quarterfinals
item.description: SOUTH BEND, Ind. (AP) — Notre Dame’s defense set a dominating tone early in its College Football Playoff opener against Indiana. The Fighting Irish stuffed the highest-scoring team in the CFP field for a 14-3 halftime lead, but then Notre Dame suffered a gut punch.
item.dc:creator: CURT RALLO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: LeBron James trolled for saying Christmas belongs to NBA after NFL viewership is revealed
item.description: LeBron James declared Christmas "our day," but the NFL had nearly 20 million more viewers than the NBA throughout the holiday this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dodgers' Mookie Betts shares message to Yankees fans who tried to pry ball out of his glove in World Series
item.description: Los Angeles Dodgers right fielder Mookie Betts revealed his message for the the Yankees fans who tried to pry the ball out of his glove during Game 4 of the World Series.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former NCAA quarterback Tommy Lazzaro dies in 'tragic' accident at US Air Force base
item.description: Tommy Lazzaro, an Army sergeant and former quarterback for the Central Michigan Chippewas, has died following an accident at Eglin Air Force Base in Florida. He was 27.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, Fox 5

item.title: Cathedral Catholic goalie shows why she's a keeper; honors her coach through art
item.description: Cathedral Catholic's 4-year starting goalie Maria'O Brien expresses her love to her head soccer coach through art. Suprising and touching longtime Cathedral Coach Dawn Lee.
item.dc:creator: Bri Savant, Fox 5

item.title: Bears lose rain-soaked rock fight to the Seahawks, drop franchise record-tying 10th straight game
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The Chicago Bears final home game of the season against the Seattle Seahawks was offense optional, as rain poured down on Soldier Field in what amounted to a cold, wet rock fight. "I thought special teams played very well.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: At least 65 million tune in for Netflix NFL Christmas Day games. NBA holiday ratings also skyrocket
item.description: LeBron James and the NBA are going to have make room for the NFL on Christmas. That shouldn't be a problem. Both leagues were winners on Wednesday. Netflix set records as the most-streamed NFL games in U.S. history while the NBA had its best holiday numbers in five years according to Nielsen.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY and TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: DIRECTV Holiday Bowl: What to know before going
item.description: It will be the first time the Holiday Bowl is held in Mission Valley — where it had been for more than 40 years — since before the pandemic. The two most recent bowl games were played in Petco Park.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Heat president Pat Riley shuts down Jimmy Butler trade rumors, addressing 'distraction' head on
item.description: Miami Heat president Pat Riley addressed rumors that the organization is willing to trade six-time All-Star Jimmy Butler, shutting them down as they've become a "distraction.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why Netflix is streaming NFL games on Christmas Day
item.description: To make the events even more enthralling, Mariah Carey is set to perform ahead of kick-off while Beyoncé is taking over halftime of the Ravens-Texans game.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Danny DeVito recalls spilling Coke on floor while attending Blazers game in 1975
item.description: Beloved Hollywood celebrity Danny DeVito appeared on former NFL wide receiver Julian Edelman’s podcast “Games with Names” on Dec. 3, where he shared a story about a night out in Portland with Jack Nicholson while the actors were filming “One Flew Over the Cuckoo’s Nest” in January of 1975.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, NewsNation

item.title: What to know if you want to follow the Buckeyes to the Rose Bowl
item.description: It will be a warm welcome for Ohio State fans traveling to Pasadena after a frigid December home game at The Shoe.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Beachy, NewsNation

item.title: Panthers use short-handed goals to beat Lightning 4-2 in opener of home-and-home set
item.description: A.J. Greer and Eetu Luostarinen scored short-handed goals 42 seconds apart in the second period to help the Florida Panthers beat the Tampa Bay Lightning 4-2 on Sunday to open a home-and-home series.
item.dc:creator: ERIK ERLENDSSON Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: O'Connell passes for 257 yards and Raiders snap 10-game skid with 19-14 win over Jaguars
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Aidan O'Connell returned from an injury and passed for 257 yards, and the Las Vegas Raiders ended a 10-game losing streak with a 19-14 victory over the Jacksonville Jaguars on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Jared Goff throws 3 TD passes as Lions beat Bears 34-17 for franchise-record 13th win
item.description: Jared Goff and the Detroit Lions locked in from the start and delivered a bounce-back performance. It came with a few team records, too.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tiger Woods and son Charlie share the lead at PNC Championship in Orlando
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — Tiger Woods and 15-year-old son Charlie ran off five straight birdies on the back nine Saturday for a 13-under 59 in the scramble format, giving them a share of the lead in the PNC Championship in Woods' first competition since back surgery in September.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Day 3: Winter Classic Basketball
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—Palisade High School was buzzing on Day 3 of the Winter Classic as the Fruita Wildcats faced off against the Palmer Ridge Bears. Fruita entered the game riding high after a recent win against Ponderosa, while Palmer Ridge looked to snap a three-game losing streak.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5
